# search


<!-- WARNING: THIS FILE WAS AUTOGENERATED! DO NOT EDIT! -->

fossick searches the web through
[`ddgs`](https://github.com/deedy5/ddgs), a metasearch library that
aggregates many backends (bing, brave, duckduckgo, startpage, mojeek,
yandex, wikipedia, …) with no API key and no Docker.
[`search()`](https://vedicreader.github.io/fossick/cli.html#search)
returns text results;
[`images()`](https://vedicreader.github.io/fossick/cli.html#images),
[`news()`](https://vedicreader.github.io/fossick/cli.html#news) and
[`videos()`](https://vedicreader.github.io/fossick/cli.html#videos)
return the corresponding media.

Direct Google is soft-blocked for many IPs (plain HTTP gets a
JavaScript-only shell), so
[`google()`](https://vedicreader.github.io/fossick/search.html#google)
uses scrapling’s stealth browser to get real Google ranking when you
specifically need it. Every result is a plain dict, and all functions
share a local TTL cache so repeated queries return instantly.

## Text, image, news & video search


``` python
def get_ddgs():
```

*Call self as a function.*

## Google (stealth browser)

Direct Google is soft-blocked for many IPs — plain HTTP (curl/requests,
and ddgs’s own
[`google`](https://vedicreader.github.io/fossick/search.html#google)
backend) gets a JavaScript-only shell with zero parseable results,
regardless of consent cookies or UA.
[`google()`](https://vedicreader.github.io/fossick/search.html#google)
sidesteps this with scrapling’s **stealth browser**
(`fetch(..., stealthy=True)`): it executes the page JS and evades bot
detection, so it gets a real SERP. It’s slower (a browser launches per
query), so it’s an opt-in source for when you need Google’s own ranking
— results are TTL-cached to amortise the cost.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/vedicreader/fossick/blob/main/fossick/search.py#L69"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### google

``` python
def google(
    q:str, n:int=10, lang:str='en'
)->list:
```

*Search Google directly via a stealth browser (real Google ranking).
Returns dicts, same shape as search().*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/vedicreader/fossick/blob/main/fossick/cli.py#L349"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### research

``` python
def research(
    q:str, # search query
    n:int=5, # number of top results to read
    engine:str='search', # 'search' (ddgs metasearch) or 'google' (stealth browser ranking)
    sel:str=None, # CSS selector to narrow each page before markdown
    chars:int=4000, # max markdown chars kept per page
    auto:bool=True, # auto-escalate fetching (plain->heavy->stealthy->session) per page
    region:str='us-en', **kw
)->dict: # extra kwargs passed to fetch()
```

*Query -\> read the top `n` results -\> a cited markdown corpus. Returns
{query, sources:\[{title,href,md}\], digest}.*

## Tests

``` python
# empty query is a no-op for every entry point (no network)
assert search('') == []

# Google SERP parser works on a static fixture (no browser/network)
_serp = ('<html><body><div class="MjjYud"><div class="g">'
         '<a href="https://ex.com/p"><h3>Example Page</h3></a>'
         '<div class="VwiC3b">A short snippet.</div></div></div>'
         '<div><a href="https://www.google.com/aclk"><h3>Ad</h3></a></div></body></html>')
_g = _parse_google(_serp, 5)
assert len(_g) == 1, f'expected 1 organic result, got {len(_g)}'   # google.com ad link filtered out
assert _g[0]['href'] == 'https://ex.com/p' and _g[0]['title'] == 'Example Page'
assert _g[0]['content'] == 'A short snippet.'
```

``` python
# text search (ddgs metasearch — aggregates working backends, no Docker)
for r in search('fasthtml python web framework', n=5): print(r['title'] , '---', r['href'])
```

``` python
# real Google ranking via stealth browser (slower; use when you need Google specifically)
for r in google('vedicreader website', n=5): print(r['title'], '—', r['href'])
```

``` python
# research(): search -> read the top n results -> one cited markdown corpus
res = research('what is the raft consensus algorithm', n=3)
assert set(res) == {'query', 'sources', 'digest'}, res.keys()
assert res['query'] and isinstance(res['sources'], list) and res['sources']
assert all({'title','href','md'} <= set(s) for s in res['sources'])
assert res['digest']
print(len(res['sources']), 'sources |', len(res['digest']), 'chars')
```
